tasks.task_name: Find warm backpacking destination (1-2 night, mid-March)
tasks.first_domino: Google 'warm backpacking March destinations near Chicago'
tasks.hook_line: 2 days until the trip weekend — no destination yet.
tasks.reward: Unlocks flight booking and gear planning — kills the biggest unknown
nuggets: Red River Gorge (KY) 60°F mid-March
Big South Fork (TN) good for 1-night loops
Shawnee National Forest warmer than Chicago
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find a warm backpacking destination for a 1-2 night trip on March 14-15, 2026. Flying from Chicago. It's for my friend Mariano's 30th birthday weekend — he wants to do a backpacking trip before the Chicago celebration on March 19th. Need somewhere warm (ideally 50°F+), beginner-friendly trails, and accessible by a short flight or drive from Chicago. Give me 3-5 options with trail names, distances, difficulty level, expected weather, and logistics (nearest airport or drive time).

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights and search Chicago to [destination]
tasks.hook_line: Flight prices jump closer to departure — book now or pay extra.
tasks.reward: Locks in the trip logistics — one less thing hanging over you
nuggets: Last-minute flights can be 2-3x normal price
Southwest often has deals 1-2 days out
Google Flights shows price calendar
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book flights from Chicago to [destination] for March 14-15, 2026. It's a quick backpacking trip for my friend Mariano's 30th. Need outbound Friday morning/midday March 14, return late Sunday March 15 or early Monday March 16 (flexible). Show me the cheapest options on Google Flights, Southwest, and United. I care more about price than time, but want to maximize time at the trail.

tasks.task_name: Book flight from backpacking to Chicago
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights and search [destination] to Chicago
tasks.hook_line: Last flight to book — gets you from trail to birthday party.
tasks.reward: Completes the travel puzzle — ready for the celebration
nuggets: Need to arrive Chicago by March 19
Birthday celebration likely evening March 19
Direct flights save headache
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book a flight from [backpacking destination] to Chicago for Mariano's 30th birthday on March 19, 2026. The backpacking trip ends March 15-16, and the birthday celebration is March 19. I have flexibility on when I arrive in Chicago (March 16, 17, or 18) but want to minimize cost. Show me options and help me pick based on price and convenience.

tasks.task_name: Figure out REI gear rentals (backpack, tent, sleeping bag)
tasks.first_domino: Google 'REI gear rental Chicago' and open REI website
tasks.hook_line: REI does full backpacking kits — could solve the gear problem in one go.
tasks.reward: Answers whether you need to scramble for gear or just show up at REI
nuggets: REI rents full backpacking kits
Includes backpack, tent, sleeping bag, pad
Can reserve online and pick up local
tasks.system_prompt: I'm doing a 1-2 night backpacking trip March 14-15 near Chicago and don't own backpacking gear. I've heard REI rents full backpacking kits. Help me figure out: (1) What REI locations near Chicago rent gear, (2) What's included in a typical kit and how much it costs, (3) Whether I need to reserve ahead or can walk in, (4) Any alternatives if REI doesn't work out. I need backpack, tent, sleeping bag, sleeping pad at minimum.

tasks.first_domino: Text Mariano: 'What do you want for your birthday?'
tasks.hook_line: A thoughtful gift beats showing up empty-handed to a 30th.
tasks.reward: Checks the gift box — shows up to the party ready
nuggets: 30th birthdays often appreciate experiences over things
Chicago has great food/drink gift options
Trip itself could count as the gift
tasks.system_prompt: My friend Mariano is turning 30 on March 19, 2026. I'm already doing a backpacking trip with him March 14-15 and going to Chicago for his birthday. Should I get him a separate gift? If yes, what's a good gift for a 30th birthday for someone who likes outdoor activities, travel, and good food/drink? Budget flexible but thinking $50-150. Give me 5-7 specific gift ideas with links if possible.

tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Google 'best eyeglasses stores Kyoto English friendly'
tasks.hook_line: Japanese glasses are cheaper and better quality — which Kyoto shops speak English?
tasks.reward: Adds glasses shopping to your Kyoto itinerary — one less thing to figure out later
nuggets: JINS and Zoff are major chains with English
Kyoto has optical shops near Kawaramachi
Can get 4 pairs for under $400 total
tasks.system_prompt: I'm going to Kyoto March 25-27, 2026 and want to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses early in the trip. I need to find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto that: (1) are English-friendly, (2) have good selection and quality, (3) can do prescriptions quickly (ideally same-day or next-day), (4) are reasonably priced. Give me a list of 5-7 stores with addresses, Google Maps links, price ranges, and notes on English service. Include both chains (JINS, Zoff) and local shops if they're good.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance: 'Can I submit foreign eyeglass receipts?'
tasks.hook_line: Can you get US insurance to cover Japanese glasses and skip the markup?
tasks.reward: Confirms whether you can save $400+ or need to adjust the plan
nuggets: Vision insurance often covers 1-2 pairs per year
Need itemized receipt in English for submission
Prescription valid across countries
tasks.system_prompt: I'm buying 4 pairs of eyeglasses in Japan in late March 2026. I have US vision insurance. Help me figure out: (1) Can I submit foreign eyeglass receipts for insurance reimbursement? (2) What documentation do I need (itemized receipt, prescription copy, etc.)? (3) Do I need to bring my current prescription or can Japanese opticians do a new eye exam? (4) Any issues using a US prescription in Japan? Draft an email to my insurance asking these questions clearly.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples worth visiting'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free entry, open 24hrs
Arashiyama bamboo grove best before 9am
Kinkaku-ji ¥500 entry, opens 9am
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. Also factor in that I need to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses early in the trip.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Takayama day trips and things to do'
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the mountain town everyone says to visit but nobody explains why.
tasks.reward: Unlocks the Takayama leg — gets you ready for the Alps
nuggets: Takayama old town is walkable in 2-3 hours
Shirakawa-go day trip is the main draw
Famous for sake breweries and morning markets
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 1.5-2 days in Takayama, Japan (late March/early April 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like outdoor activities, walking, traditional villages, and local food. I've heard Shirakawa-go is a must-do day trip. Give me a suggested itinerary with: (1) What to do in Takayama itself (old town, sake breweries, markets), (2) Whether Shirakawa-go is worth it and how to get there, (3) Any other nearby day trips or hikes, (4) Food recommendations.

tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive — which neighborhoods match your vibe vs tourist traps?
tasks.reward: Finishes all activity planning — trip is fully mapped
nuggets: Tokyo has 20+ distinct neighborhoods
Shibuya, Shinjuku, Harajuku all walkable cluster
TeamLab Borderless needs advance tickets
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 5-6 days in Tokyo (early April 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like: outdoor spaces, interesting neighborhoods, good food, art/culture, and some shopping (fountain pens, watches). Not interested in: theme parks, nightlife clubs, super touristy stuff. Give me a day-by-day framework with neighborhood clusters, specific places to visit, and notes on what's worth pre-booking. Include time for Dana's 30th birthday on April 6.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open README.md
tasks.hook_line: Can you crack the local setup before the next team sync?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all future Cerebro work — no more waiting on staging
nuggets: Developer portals often need Docker + Node
README usually has quickstart guide
Local setup reveals real dev workflow
tasks.system_prompt: I'm trying to get Cerebro (our internal developer portal) running locally. It's been parked for a while because I haven't set it up on my machine yet. I need help with: (1) What dependencies are typically needed for a developer portal (Docker, Node, databases?), (2) How to interpret the README and get past common setup blockers, (3) What to do if the README is outdated or incomplete, (4) How to validate it's actually working once it starts. I'm comfortable with command line but want to avoid rabbit holes.

recommended_slots.reason: Most urgent: trip is in 2 days and destination is still unknown — blocks all other trip logistics
Quick research win that sets up Kyoto shopping early in the Japan trip — low effort, clear output
Batches well with slot 1 (same trip context) and answers gear question before weekend departure
